sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 21.3g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
sections.subtitleText: 21.3g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 4.1g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, this is doing the right stuff.
sections.subtitleText: 4.1g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for a dairy food, this is doing the right stuff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 14.2g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
sections.subtitleText: 14.2g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 27% daily value, vitamin a at 14% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin b12 at 27% daily value, vitamin a at 14% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: calcium at 37% daily value, zinc at 19% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: calcium at 37% daily value, zinc at 19% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: easy to fit into familiar meals because that lowers friction for regular use. can be a very convenient protein vehicle because convenience is one of dairy’s strongest practical advantages. slots into meals very easily because practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not. not everyone tolerates dairy well because that is a real use-case limitation. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story.

closing.summary: Feta does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while fats are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
